page: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8175M processors. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core processing and memory subsystem performance. The server achieved a multi-core stress-ng score of 81870.14 and a Passmark CPU score of 57080.9, both placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 4545.56 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 222.69 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database and in-memory operations are highly performant, as demonstrated by a Redis SET throughput of 74.16 million operations per second and a Passmark database operations score of 21974.43.

While the server excels in memory-intensive workloads and multi-threaded CPU tasks, it exhibits limitations in single-core performance and machine learning inference. Single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall in the average range, and LLM text generation speeds are weak to poor, with a 7B model generating only 5.25 tokens per second due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Given these characteristics, the r5.metal is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing workloads that benefit from direct bare-metal access without virtualization overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8175M processors running at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, offering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The server delivers strong multi-core and memory performance, highlighted by a multi-core stress-ng score of 81870.14 and a Redis SET throughput of 74.16 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation is weak due to the absence of dedicated accelerators. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring direct hardware access.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for large-scale databases and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs and 768 GB DDR4 RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal server featuring 768 GB of DDR4 memory and 96 vCPUs for demanding in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: Bare-metal architecture with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon cores
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory optimized for large-scale in-memory workloads
Strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth capabilities
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for reliable connectivity
Best suited for enterprise databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications
